input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use an MSBuild condition based on the `IsTestProject` property (set automatically when a project references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` or uses the MSTest SDK), or fall back to a naming convention. Here's the recommended pattern in your `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<Project>
  <!-- Shared properties for all projects go here -->

  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**How it works:** `IsTestProject` is set to `true` by the .NET SDK when the project references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`. Your library projects won't have that reference, so the block is skipped for them.

**Fallback — naming convention:** If `IsTestProject` isn't reliably set in your repo, condition on the project name instead:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
```

The `IsTestProject` approach is preferred because it's convention-independent and automatically correct for any project that's actually a test project.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a knowledge question — no need to explore a codebase. I can answer directly.

The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for the presence of the MSTest package reference or a convention like the project name ending in `.Tests`. The most robust approach is to use `IsTestProject` property or condition on the project name.
